refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a KPI requirement that must be operationalized with measurable acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target), explicit duty cycle definition, and telemetry-based verification. The refined FR clarifies that cycle time is a system-level performance outcome dependent on vehicle dynamics, operator control responsiveness, and environmental factors. High safety criticality tailoring requires that cycle time optimization does not compromise safety interlocks or emergency response.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure p95 cycle time across ≥100 representative load→travel→dump cycles under agreed duty cycle (load mass, terrain, distance, ambient conditions). Verify telemetry capture of cycle phase timestamps. Confirm no safety alerts or emergency stops artificially inflate cycle time during measurement window.
sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump phases with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall provide operator control responsiveness sufficient to meet cycle time target without introducing safety-critical latency.
FR-2.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall enforce safety interlocks without blocking nominal cycle progression.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall capture and report cycle phase telemetry (timestamps, phase transitions, cycle duration) for KPI verification.

candidate_dps: DP-2.1: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/electric actuation timing, reduce phase transition delays, and tune operator control loop bandwidth to minimize cycle duration while maintaining safety margins.
DP-2.2: Implement predictive cycle scheduling and parallel phase execution (e.g., travel during dump preparation) to reduce sequential bottlenecks.
DP-2.3: Deploy low-latency teleoperation network stack and operator interface with predictive input buffering to reduce control-to-action delay.
DP-2.4: Establish cycle time telemetry pipeline with sub-second phase timestamp resolution and automated p95 KPI calculation.
selected_dp.selected_dp: Integrated approach: (1) Vehicle actuation and phase timing optimization (DP-2.1) as primary lever; (2) Low-latency teleoperation control loop (DP-2.3) to minimize operator-induced delays; (3) Cycle telemetry instrumentation and KPI reporting (DP-2.4) to enable closed-loop performance verification and continuous improvement. Parallel phase execution (DP-2.2) deferred to detailed design pending safety analysis.

potential_sources: Vehicle actuation timing (DP-2.1) is decoupled from teleoperation latency (DP-2.3) via control interface abstraction; both feed into telemetry (DP-2.4).
Safety Services must validate that phase timing optimization does not violate load stability or emergency stop response time; this is a constraint, not a coupling.
Telemetry pipeline (DP-2.4) is independent of actuation and control; it observes and reports.
coupling_check.notes: No circular dependencies detected. DP-2.1 and DP-2.3 are parallel design threads that converge on the telemetry interface. Safety criticality is managed via constraint enforcement, not functional coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Vehicle actuation hardware (hydraulics/electric motors) can achieve target phase timing without redesign; risk: legacy hardware may have inherent delays.
Assumption: Network infrastructure supports low-latency teleoperation (e.g., <100 ms round-trip); risk: site conditions (RF interference, distance) may degrade performance.
Assumption: Cycle time target CT_target is achievable under agreed duty cycle; risk: target may be unrealistic for given load/terrain/distance.
Risk: Optimizing for cycle time may create pressure to disable or bypass safety interlocks; mitigation required in Safety Services design.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-2.1 to explicitly state safety constraints: 'Vehicle actuation timing shall be optimized subject to load stability margins, emergency stop response time ≤ T_emergency, and all safety interlocks remaining active.' Clarify CT_target value and duty cycle parameters in requirements baseline before detailed design.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Owns vehicle actuation timing optimization (DP-2.1), phase sequencing logic, and cycle phase instrumentation. Responsible for delivering deterministic load→travel→dump cycle performance within design envelope. Interfaces with Teleoperation for control commands and Safety Services for safety constraint enforcement.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives cycle phase commands (load, travel, dump) from Teleoperation or autonomous planner; triggers phase transitions.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Emits cycle phase timestamps, phase duration, actuation state, and load/position telemetry for KPI calculation and diagnostics.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety constraint signals from Safety Services (e.g., load stability warnings, emergency stop); Vehicle must respect these signals and halt/abort phases as required.

module_containment_scores.notes: Vehicle is the natural owner of actuation timing, phase sequencing, and cycle instrumentation. Cycle time is fundamentally a vehicle performance metric. Minimal diffusion: only telemetry export and safety signal ingestion cross boundaries.

Teleoperation owns operator control responsiveness (DP-2.3) but does not own vehicle actuation or phase timing. Assigning cycle time KPI to Teleoperation would diffuse responsibility and create false coupling between control latency and vehicle hardware performance.

Safety Services enforces constraints on cycle timing (e.g., emergency stop response) but does not own the optimization or measurement of cycle time. Assigning cycle time KPI to Safety Services would conflate safety constraint enforcement with performance optimization.

NGHE Solution is the system-level integrator and owns the overall KPI target, but the realization of cycle time performance is delegated to Vehicle subsystem. Assigning IM to NGHE Solution would obscure the Vehicle's responsibility for actuation and phase timing.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Vehicle module contains the actuation hardware, phase sequencing logic, and cycle instrumentation required to realize DP-2.1 and DP-2.4. Teleoperation and Safety Services interact with Vehicle via well-defined interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert) without diffusing cycle time responsibility.
containment_risks: Risk: If Vehicle module does not have authority to optimize actuation timing (e.g., if timing is hard-coded in legacy firmware), then DP-2.1 cannot be realized; mitigation: confirm Vehicle design authority and firmware update capability.

Risk: If telemetry instrumentation is not built into Vehicle (e.g., phase timestamps are calculated post-hoc in a separate analytics module), then cycle time measurement may be inaccurate; mitigation: ensure Vehicle emits phase transition events with hardware-synchronized timestamps.

Risk: If Safety Services constraints are not clearly communicated to Vehicle (e.g., via SafetyAlert interface), then Vehicle may optimize cycle time in ways that violate safety margins; mitigation: formalize SafetyAlert signal contract and validate Vehicle's respect for constraints.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Confirm that Vehicle module has design authority over actuation timing and firmware update capability. Specify SafetyAlert interface contract (signal names, timing, priority) to ensure Vehicle respects safety constraints during cycle time optimization. Verify that Vehicle telemetry includes sub-second phase transition timestamps synchronized to a common clock.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR (achieve p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target with telemetry verification) is directly addressed by selected DP (vehicle actuation optimization + teleoperation latency reduction + telemetry instrumentation). DP is sufficient and necessary to satisfy FR. No functional gaps or over-specification detected.

diffusion_risks: Teleoperation latency (DP-2.3) is owned by Teleoperation module, not Vehicle; ensure Teleoperation and Vehicle interfaces are well-defined and latency budgets are allocated.
Telemetry pipeline (DP-2.4) may require coordination between Vehicle (phase timestamp generation) and NGHE Solution (KPI calculation); ensure ownership and interface clarity.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module cleanly contains DP-2.1 (actuation timing) and contributes to DP-2.4 (telemetry generation). DP-2.3 (teleoperation latency) is owned by Teleoperation module and interfaces with Vehicle via Tasking. No significant diffusion detected; module boundaries are appropriate.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Cycle time KPI is measurable via Vehicle telemetry (phase timestamps). Verification plan is clear: measure p95 across ≥100 cycles under agreed duty cycle. Safety criticality is managed via Safety Services constraints, not by Vehicle module directly.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Vehicle is clearly responsible for actuation timing and phase sequencing. Teleoperation is responsible for control responsiveness. Safety Services is responsible for constraint enforcement. NGHE Solution is responsible for system-level KPI target and integration. Allocation is clear and non-overlapping.

recommended_revisions: Formalize CT_target value and duty cycle parameters (load mass, terrain type, travel distance, ambient conditions) in requirements baseline before detailed design.
Define SafetyAlert interface contract (signal names, timing, priority) to ensure Vehicle respects safety constraints during cycle time optimization.
Specify telemetry interface contract for phase transition events (timestamp resolution, synchronization method, data format) to enable accurate KPI calculation.
Allocate latency budgets across Teleoperation and Vehicle to ensure combined control-to-action delay does not exceed cycle time target.

decision.justification: Refined FR-2 is well-formed and decomposed into four sub-FRs (vehicle actuation, teleoperation control, safety constraint enforcement, telemetry instrumentation). Selected DP is sound and addresses all sub-FRs without coupling. Vehicle module is the appropriate IM boundary with high containment score (0.95) and minimal diffusion. Gate review passes all three checks. Proceed to next decomposition level to detail vehicle actuation timing optimization (FR-2.1), teleoperation control loop design (FR-2.2), safety interlock logic (FR-2.3), and telemetry pipeline architecture (FR-2.4).
next_level_sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump phases with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall provide operator control responsiveness sufficient to meet cycle time target without introducing safety-critical latency.
FR-2.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall enforce safety interlocks without blocking nominal cycle progression.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall capture and report cycle phase telemetry (timestamps, phase transitions, cycle duration) for KPI verification.

assumptions: Vehicle actuation hardware (hydraulics/electric motors) can achieve target phase timing without major redesign.
Network infrastructure supports low-latency teleoperation (e.g., <100 ms round-trip).
Cycle time target CT_target is achievable under agreed duty cycle.
Safety Services constraints can be communicated to Vehicle via SafetyAlert interface without blocking nominal cycle progression.
Vehicle module has design authority over actuation timing and firmware update capability.
open_risks: Legacy vehicle hardware may have inherent delays that prevent achieving CT_target; mitigation: conduct feasibility study on actuation timing.
Site network conditions may degrade teleoperation latency; mitigation: characterize network performance and establish fallback modes.
CT_target may be unrealistic for given load/terrain/distance; mitigation: validate target against physics-based cycle time model.
Safety constraint enforcement may create bottlenecks in cycle progression; mitigation: detail safety interlock logic in FR-2.3 decomposition.
Telemetry instrumentation may introduce measurement errors or latency; mitigation: specify timestamp synchronization and validation methods in FR-2.4 decomposition.
evidence_needed: Confirmation of Vehicle module design authority and firmware update capability.
Specification of CT_target value and duty cycle parameters (load mass, terrain, distance, ambient conditions).
Network latency characterization for teleoperation link under site conditions.
Physics-based cycle time model to validate feasibility of CT_target.
SafetyAlert interface contract (signal names, timing, priority).
Telemetry interface specification (phase transition events, timestamp resolution, synchronization method).
Latency budget allocation across Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
